name: session-install-workflow
description: Required interactive workflow for installing agent-session templates from the default remote repository.
reason: User added a desktop refresh step after session template installation.
content:
Context file: session-install-workflow
Description: Required interactive workflow for installing agent-session templates from the default remote repository.
Last updated by: opencode-agent
Reason: User added a desktop refresh step after session template installation.

<session-install-workflow>
When the user asks to install a session or install an agent-session template, follow this workflow unless the user explicitly overrides it.

Repository and template discovery:
- Use the default agent repository from the default-agent-repository context unless the user explicitly provides another repository URL or local repository path.
- First fetch or inspect a repository-level session/template index file from the remote repository. This index file does not exist yet; its schema will be defined later with the user. Until it exists, fall back to discovering templates under agents/ in the repository and inspecting each package's agent.json.
- Present the available templates to the user and ask which template to install. Do not install a template before the user selects one.

Workspace decision:
- After the user selects a template, ask whether to create/use a new workspace or use an existing workspace.
- If the user chooses a new workspace, ask for the workspace directory path. Use that directory as the target workspace directory for the new session metadata/configuration.
- If the user chooses an existing workspace, list known workspaces discovered from the opencode HTTP API session list.

How to discover existing workspaces:
- Prefer the opencode HTTP API over scanning session metadata files.
- Read the local desktop sidecar JSON from the manager session metadata, then call GET /session with Basic Auth and x-opencode-directory set to the relevant manager/global directory.
- Treat each distinct session.directory as a workspace. If directory is missing, fall back to session.path, then session.projectID.
- For each workspace, show a concise label using directory, projectID if useful, and the count of sessions found for that workspace.
- Sort workspaces by latest activity descending, preferring session.time.updated, then session.time.created.
- Do not scan Global.Path.data/session/*/metadata.json for routine workspace discovery unless the HTTP API is unavailable or the user explicitly asks for file-level inspection.

Session decision for an existing workspace:
- After the user selects an existing workspace, ask whether to create a new session from the selected template or overwrite an existing session in that workspace.
- If creating a new session, ask the user for the new session name/title. Create or identify the new session, then install/overwrite the selected template's managed files into that new session.
- If overwriting an existing session, ask the user to choose the target session under that workspace before calling manage-session install with overwrite true.

How to list sessions under a workspace:
- Use the opencode HTTP API session list and filter sessions whose directory matches the selected workspace directory. If directory was unavailable for workspace discovery, use the same fallback key used to select the workspace.
- Sort sessions by latest activity descending, preferring session.time.updated, then session.time.created.
- If the workspace has many sessions, list only the 10 most recently active sessions first and explicitly tell the user these are the 10 most recent sessions.
- If the desired target session is not in the first 10, either continue listing more sessions or ask the user for identifying information such as session id, title, directory, or approximate time, then search for it in the API results.


Desktop refresh after install:
- After creating or updating the target session and installing the selected template, dispatch a frontend navigation request to "classic" with session-dispatch to refresh the desktop UI state.
- If the user is working in the manager page and should continue there, optionally dispatch back to "manager" after the classic navigation.
- This refresh step is for frontend visibility only; do not treat it as install verification. Still verify the target session files directly when needed.

Safety rules:
- Do not overwrite any existing session until the user has explicitly selected the target session and confirmed the overwrite path.
- Before editing the target session configuration, inspect that session's metadata.json and existing context/system/tool files. This inspection is for the selected target only, not for broad discovery.
- Report the selected template, target workspace, target session id, and files changed after installation.
</session-install-workflow>
